remember: Students will be able to recall the definition and features of a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to identify the different parts of a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to describe the characteristics of a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to list the different types of bills of exchange.
understand: Students will be able to explain the process of drawing, acceptance, and discounting of a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to describe the concept of maturity date and its importance in a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to explain the different types of holders of a bill of exchange and their rights.
Students will be able to describe the consequences of failure to meet the terms of a bill of exchange.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the amount of discount on a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to determine the interest on a bill of exchange.
Students will be able to prepare a bill of exchange and its related documents.
Students will be able to analyze the pros and cons of using a bill of exchange for payment.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and disadvantages of using a bill of exchange for payment.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the bill of exchange on the cash flow of a business.
Students will be able to compare the different types of bills of exchange and their uses.
Students will be able to suggest alternative methods of payment instead of using a bill of exchange.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the concept of a bill of exchange and its features.
To describe the process of drawing, acceptance, and discounting of a bill of exchange.
To explain the different types of bills of exchange and their uses.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the different parts of a bill of exchange and their significance.
To calculate the amount of discount on a bill of exchange and interest thereon.
To prepare a bill of exchange and its related documents, and analyze its pros and cons.
